seoTitle: How to Get a Brevo API Key Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to generate, name, and securely store a Brevo API key for connecting website forms, plugins, and CRM integrations.
intro: Connecting a website form, automation plugin, or CRM to Brevo requires a working API key, and finding where to generate one isn't always obvious. This guide walks through Brevo's SMTP and API settings to create a new key, name it for tracking purposes, and copy the credential before it disappears from view. It's a quick reference for anyone setting up their first integration.

steps.heading: Open your account settings
steps.body: Click your profile name in the top right corner of Brevo and select SMTP and API from the menu.

steps.heading: Access the API keys panel
steps.body: Click into the API Keys and MCP tab to open your credential dashboard.

steps.heading: Generate a new key
steps.body: Click Generate a new API key, type a clear descriptive name for your integration, and set your preferred expiration boundary.

steps.heading: Copy your key immediately
steps.body: Click Generate, then use the copy icon right away since the full token only displays once.

steps.heading: Store it securely
steps.body: Paste your credential into a password manager or directly into your website integration fields, then return to the key list to confirm it's active.

faq.question: Can I see my API key again after I close the popup?
faq.answer: No, the full key only displays once during creation, so you need to copy it immediately or generate a new one if you lose it.

faq.question: Can I set an expiration date on my API key?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure expiration boundaries when generating the key to limit how long it stays active.

faq.question: How do I deactivate a compromised API key?
faq.answer: Return to the API Keys dashboard where you can use instant deactivation tools to pause or replace any key.

faq.question: Should each integration have its own API key?
faq.answer: Yes, assigning distinct descriptive names to separate integrations makes it easier to track and manage external systems individually.
